title: Introduction: Automated Literature Review Generation
objective: Contextualize the problem of manual literature review bottleneck and introduce the motivation for automated text summarization approaches in scientific document processing

title: NLP Approaches for Literature Review Automation
objective: Present and compare the three implemented methodologies: extractive (spaCy frequency-based), abstractive (T5 fine-tuned), and LLM-based (GPT-3.5 with RAG) approaches

title: Evaluation Framework and Dataset Limitations
objective: Analyze the reliance on SciTLDR dataset and ROUGE metrics, examining how single-dataset evaluation constrains generalizability claims

title: Single-Document vs. Multi-Document Summarization
objective: Examine the distinction between TLDR generation (single-document) and literature review synthesis (multi-document integration), addressing the methodological conflation in the evaluated approach

title: Human-AI Collaboration and Interactive Refinement
objective: Discuss the absence of frameworks for user feedback integration and interactive refinement in the current literature review automation pipeline

title: Multilingual and Cross-Domain Generalization
objective: Address the critical gap in evaluating approaches across non-English scientific documents and diverse research domains
